item.text: I’m going to locate all call-sites of `orbit_tools::ToolRegistry::register` using only repository search tools, then verify each hit with focused file reads so we only return true invocations (not definitions or other `register` methods).

I found broad matches, including benchmark artifacts and other registry types. Next I’ll restrict to `crates/` and inspect each candidate file to confirm the receiver is actually `orbit_tools::ToolRegistry`.
I’ve narrowed this to candidate files in `crates/`; now I’m verifying each one’s `registry` type declaration to separate true `ToolRegistry` call-sites from other registries.

I’ve confirmed the `orbit-tools` builtin modules use `&mut ToolRegistry`. I’ll quickly validate the remaining hits are different registry types and check the `ToolRegistry` definition file so we can build the exact include/exclude sets.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'for f in $(rg --files crates); do if rg -q "'"\\.register\\(\" \""'$f" && rg -q "ToolRegistry" "$f"; then echo "$f"; fi; done | sort -u'
/bin/zsh -lc 'for f in $(rg --files crates); do if rg -q "'"\\.register\\(\" \""'$f" && rg -q "ToolRegistry" "$f"; then echo "$f"; fi; done | sort -u'
